Gabe Newell of Valve was particularly outspoken about the system, calling it a "total disaster on so many levels". While other developers adapted over time, Valve were forced to use another studio to port Portal 2 onto the PS3.
error: Valve were forced to use another studio to port Portal 2 onto the PS3
correction: Um, actually, Valve built the PS3 version themselves. Newell even took the stage at Sony's E3 2010 keynote to announce it, calling the Steamworks-enabled PS3 release the best console version.
title: PlayStation 3

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The late 90s and early 2000s saw the broadcast of the highly popular original run of Robot Wars. Across its series the show birthed many iconic competing robots, including the two-time champion and powerful flipper-based Chaos 2, the spinning-disc Hypno-Disc, the piercing Razer, which holds the record for the most wins, and Dead Metal, whose pincers held robots in place for its circular saw.
error: Dead Metal, whose pincers held robots in place for its circular saw
correction: Um, actually, Dead Metal was never a competitor. It was one of the show's House Robots, part of the arena's hazards used to punish competing machines rather than an entrant itself.
title: Robot Wars

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Spielberg's Jaws had a significant effect on the film industry, often cited as the first summer blockbuster. The film is set on the fictional Amity Island around the 4th of July. To hunt down the shark, Police Captain Brody, marine biologist Hooper and shark hunter Quint board the Orca. Initial tension between the three men gives way to camaraderie as their brushes with the shark - and a round of swapping scar stories - cause them to bond.
error: Police Captain Brody
correction: Um, actually, Brody's title is Chief, not Captain. Martin Brody is the Chief of Police of Amity Island, a former NYPD officer who took the small-town job to escape big-city crime.
title: Jaws

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: As the McCallister family prepares to spend Christmas in Paris, Kevin is sent to the attic as punishment for ruining dinner. He wakes the next morning to find the house empty and believes his wish for his family to disappear has come true. While home alone, the McCallister house is targeted by Henry and Marv, the Wet Bandits, who have been breaking into other vacant houses in the neighbourhood.
error: Henry and Marv
correction: Um, actually, the burglars are Harry and Marv. Harry, played by Joe Pesci, and Marv, played by Daniel Stern, earn the "Wet Bandits" nickname by leaving the taps running in the houses they rob.
title: Home Alone

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen, the Decepticon Devastator is formed when the six Constructicons merge together, using their combined strength to climb the Great Pyramid of Giza and reveal the Star Harvester hidden inside, before being destroyed by the Navy's railgun.
error: the six Constructicons
correction: Um, actually, the official count is seven Constructicons. Hasbro's canon and the concept art list seven, though the film itself is famously inconsistent about exactly how many combine into Devastator.
title: Transformers

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In Uncharted 4: A Thief's End, Nathan Drake, his brother Sam, his mentor Sully and his girlfriend Elena Fisher set out to track down the pirate utopia of Libertalia. After trips to Italy, Scotland and Madagascar - and some separation - the group reunites on Libertalia and uncovers the civil war that unfolded there, which killed all of the pirates.
error: his girlfriend Elena Fisher
correction: Um, actually, Elena is Nate's wife by this point. The two married between Uncharted 2 and 3, and A Thief's End opens with them living together as a married couple.
title: Uncharted

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Disney currently owns and operates 6 resorts across the world under the Disney Experiences umbrella. Disneyland Resort was the original, in Anaheim, California. Walt Disney World opened in 1971 in Florida. Tokyo Disney Resort opened in 1983 as the first international resort. Disneyland Paris, as it is now known, opened in 1992. Hong Kong Disneyland Resort and Shanghai Disney Resort opened in 2005 and 2016 respectively.
error: Disney currently owns and operates 6 resorts
correction: Um, actually, Disney does not own and operate all six. Tokyo Disney Resort is owned and run by the Oriental Land Company under licence from Disney - the only Disney resort Disney does not operate itself.
title: Disneyland

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The first two games in the RCT series were developed almost single-handedly by Chris Sawyer, with only the graphics and music made by others. The third game, developed by Frontier, ditched the iconic isometric style for a full 3D view, letting the player ride all of the game's coasters and flat rides as one of the park's visitors.
error: ride all of the game's coasters and flat rides
correction: Um, actually, the first-person camera only worked on tracked rides. You could ride coasters and other tracked attractions, but not non-tracked flat rides such as motion simulators.
title: RollerCoaster Tycoon

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In Super Mario Galaxy, the mustachioed plumber boards the Starship Rosalina. From here Mario is launched to a number of galaxies to collect their Grand Stars. Controlled by Rosalina, it serves as home to both her and the Lumas, one of whom transformed into a beacon, giving the hub the power to travel across the universe.
error: Starship Rosalina
correction: Um, actually, the hub is called the Comet Observatory. The name Starship Mario belongs to the hub in the sequel, Super Mario Galaxy 2, not the first game.
title: Super Mario Galaxy

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: As Ezio progresses through Assassin's Creed II, he gains access to new weapons and tools, including the hidden blade, a poison-tipped blade and, eventually, a crossbow that allows for silent ranged kills. These upgrades come mainly from Leonardo da Vinci, who deciphers Altair's Codex pages and improves Ezio's equipment throughout the story.
error: a crossbow that allows for silent ranged kills
correction: Um, actually, Ezio doesn't get a crossbow in Assassin's Creed II. The weapon wasn't introduced to the series until the sequel, Assassin's Creed: Brotherhood.
title: Assassin's Creed

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Joey Tribbiani is an aspiring actor best known for playing Dr. Drake Ramoray on the soap opera "Days of Our Lives". He first gets the role through an audition Monica helps him prepare for, and becomes a fan favourite until the writers kill off his character by throwing him down an elevator shaft. After months of unemployment, he returns to the show when the writers bring back Drake as his twin brother, a neurosurgeon named Striker Ramoray.
error: as his twin brother, a neurosurgeon named Striker Ramoray
correction: Um, actually, Joey returns as Drake himself, not a twin. The soap revives the character with a far-fetched brain-transplant storyline rather than by inventing a new sibling.
title: Friends

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The Kraken is a legendary sea monster believed to originate in Scandinavian folklore, often depicted as a giant octopus or squid capable of dragging entire ships under the sea. Early sailors reported sightings near Greenland and Norway, describing whirlpools and massive tentacles. Some light was shed on the legend when enormous squid carcasses began washing up on Nordic shores in the 1800s, suggesting the Kraken was likely based on the now-extinct giant squids of the North Atlantic.
error: the now-extinct giant squids of the North Atlantic
correction: Um, actually, giant squid are not extinct. The species is alive and well today - the IUCN lists it as 'least concern' - it is simply an elusive deep-sea animal that humans rarely observe.
title: The Kraken

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Road Runner's incredible speed is a defining feature of his character. In the cartoons he runs so fast that he can outrun rockets, cars and even gravity itself. The speed is part of the comedic tension, as Wile E. Coyote's traps are often rendered useless simply because the Road Runner is so much quicker. In fact, Road Runner is said to be able to run at speeds exceeding 250 miles per hour, faster than any animal known to man.
error: said to be able to run at speeds exceeding 250 miles per hour
correction: Um, actually, the classic cartoons never give a figure like this. The Road Runner's speed is deliberately left as comedic hyperbole rather than quantified as 250 mph.
title: Looney Tunes

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: At the conclusion of Moana, she returns the Heart of Te Fiti after Maui helps her fight the lava monster Te Ka, restoring balance to the islands. Moana returns to Motunui a hero, ushering in a new era of exploration in which her people sail the seas once again.
error: Maui helps her fight the lava monster Te Ka
correction: Um, actually, Moana doesn't defeat Te Ka in combat. She realises the lava monster is Te Fiti in corrupted form and calms it with compassion, restoring the Heart so Te Ka can transform back into the goddess.
title: Moana

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The Worms games have featured a wide range of weird and wonderful weapons. Armageddon, the fourth installment in the series, released in 1999 and contained many oddities including the Homing Pigeon, Banana Bomb, Aqua Sheep, Mole Squadron, Holy Hand Grenade, Concrete Donkey and the Old Woman.
error: the fourth installment in the series
correction: Um, actually, Worms Armageddon was the third game, not the fourth. It followed the original Worms in 1995 and Worms 2 in 1997, having begun life as an expansion to Worms 2.
title: Worms Armageddon

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In Crash Bandicoot, players break crates to gain bonuses. Most crates grant Wumpa fruit, while those marked with a C provide a checkpoint within the level. Crates bearing Aku Aku's mask grant a floating mask that protects Crash from damage. Collecting three masks in a row grants Crash temporary invincibility from all damage and, while the mask is worn, lets him run through enemies.
error: temporary invincibility from all damage
correction: Um, actually, the golden Aku Aku doesn't protect against everything. Crash is immune to enemies and TNT while it's active, but he can still die by falling into a pit, dropping into water or being crushed.
title: Crash Bandicoot

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Compound V is the mysterious chemical substance created by German geneticist Frederick Vought. The serum mutates organic tissue, granting strange characteristics. In adults its effects can be highly volatile - the powers are random and carry a high risk of death. In children the compound has a much higher success rate, and depending on where the dose is administered the resulting powers can be somewhat influenced.
error: depending on where the dose is administered the resulting powers can be somewhat influenced
correction: Um, actually, the powers are always random no matter how the V is administered. Where or how you inject it doesn't shape the ability - though some people are simply predisposed to stronger powers than others.
title: The Boys

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Getting a Weird Al parody of your song is often seen by musicians as an honour. However, due to US copyright law he still needs to get approval from the artist and label before his parodies can be released. One notable blocked release was a parody of Paul McCartney's "Live and Let Die" that would have been titled "Chicken Pot Pie", reportedly turned down by McCartney over his vegetarianism - ironically, Weird Al is a strict vegetarian too.
error: due to US copyright law he still needs to get approval from the artist and label
correction: Um, actually, US law doesn't require it - parody is protected as fair use. Weird Al is free to release parodies without permission but chooses to seek an artist's blessing every time, and won't put one out without it.
title: Weird Al Yankovic

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The climactic moment of 2013's The Last of Us sees Joel and Ellie arrive in Denver, Colorado, home to the Fireflies. In the hope of producing a vaccine, Ellie is prepped for surgery - but the operation will require removing part of her brain, killing her. Unwilling to let Ellie die, Joel fights his way through the hospital to the operating room, where he kills Jerry Anderson, the head surgeon, setting off the cycle of vengeance that carries through the sequel.
error: Denver, Colorado
correction: Um, actually, the Fireflies' hospital is in Salt Lake City, Utah. Denver is only where the group had earlier abandoned a lab - the final surgery takes place at St. Mary's Hospital in Salt Lake City.
title: The Last of Us

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Fans of Bluey have spotted a number of easter eggs in the show. Chloe the Dalmatian lives at house number 101, and the Heeler's number plate contains 419, a nod to the hex code for blue. Another example is in the episode "Markets", where a 5 Australian dollar note shows the monarch depicted as a corgi - King Charles III.
error: King Charles III
correction: Um, actually, it's Queen Elizabeth II on that note. She was still the reigning monarch when the episode aired, so the corgi caricature is of the Queen, a fitting nod to her famous love of the breed.
title: Bluey

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Sauropods are a clade of dinosaurs with very long necks, small heads and four pillar-like legs. They are notable for their enormous size, and the group includes the largest and heaviest known animals to have ever lived. While Brachiosaurus and Diplodocus are arguably better known, Argentinosaurus is the largest known sauropod, measuring around 30 metres in length with an estimated weight of up to 100 tons.
error: the largest and heaviest known animals to have ever lived
correction: Um, actually, they're the longest, but not the heaviest. The living blue whale is comparable in length to Argentinosaurus yet can weigh roughly twice as much, making it the heaviest animal known to have ever lived.
title: Dinosaurs

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Residing in the town of Bedrock, Fred and Wilma Flintstone, their daughter Pebbles and their pet dinosaur Dino live next door to the Rubbles. Barney works at the same quarry as Fred, and his wife Bernie - like Wilma - has a habit of spending a lot of money and getting angry when another woman interacts with her husband.
error: his wife Bernie
correction: Um, actually, Barney's wife is Betty Rubble. Betty is Wilma Flintstone's best friend and the Flintstones' next-door neighbour, not a 'Bernie'.
title: The Flintstones

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Steven Soderbergh's Ocean's Eleven follows George Clooney's Danny Ocean as he attempts to rob a trio of Las Vegas casinos owned by Terry Benedict, the current partner of Ocean's ex-wife Tess. Ocean assembles a well-equipped team including "The Amazing Yen", a Chinese acrobat and contortionist who ends up hidden in the vault, having been smuggled in via SWAT team holdalls.
error: smuggled in via SWAT team holdalls
correction: Um, actually, Yen is smuggled into the vault inside a money trolley wheeled in by the Malloy brothers. The SWAT team gear is the crew's disguise for getting the cash out, not for getting Yen in.
title: Ocean's Eleven

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: Hercule Poirot is a fictional French detective created by Agatha Christie. The character appeared in 33 novels, two plays and 51 short stories, all written by Christie, and has seen many radio, film and TV portrayals by actors including Ian Holm, Orson Welles, David Suchet, Kenneth Branagh and John Malkovich. He is well known for his moustache and is often shown walking with a cane and a limp.
error: a fictional French detective
correction: Um, actually, Poirot is Belgian, not French. It's a running gag in Christie's stories that the fastidious detective takes great offence at being mistaken for a Frenchman.
title: Poirot

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In this Ancient Greek-based roguelike, players control Zagreus, son of Hades, as he attempts to escape the Underworld. Across his runs, Zagreus is aided by various Olympians as well as the primordial Chaos, who offer boons. Through the game's four main biomes you also encounter other iconic underworld residents, including Sisyphus, Eurydice, Patroclus, Achilles, Charon and Cerberus.
error: Sisyphus, Eurydice, Patroclus, Achilles, Charon and Cerberus
correction: Um, actually, Achilles isn't found out in the biomes. He is Zagreus' mentor and stays in the House of Hades; only later, once reunited, does he visit Patroclus, the shade who lives in Elysium.
title: Hades

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The Nightman Cometh is regarded as one of the best episodes of Sunny. It sees Charlie write a complete musical under the secret pretense of proposing to his long-term stalking target, the Waitress. While all of the songs in the musical are written single-handedly by Charlie, Dee ad-libs a number - much to Charlie's chagrin - to assert that she does in fact "not want to have sex with a child".
error: all of the songs in the musical are written single-handedly by Charlie
correction: Um, actually, Charlie didn't write them all alone. The closing number, "Dayman", was co-written with Dennis back in the ninth episode of Season 3 before being worked into the musical.
title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: The iconic pinball game that came pre-installed on PCs from Windows 95 to Windows XP featured just one table, called Space Explorer. Rendered in glorious 640 x 480 resolution, it was a cut-down version of Full Tilt! Pinball, published by Maxis, makers of the SimCity and Sims series.
error: called Space Explorer
correction: Um, actually, the table was called Space Cadet. "3D Pinball for Windows - Space Cadet" was a single-table trimming of Full Tilt! Pinball, which also included the Skulduggery and Dragon's Keep tables.
title: 3D Pinball for Windows

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In the 1991 film, the Addams family are forced to leave their mansion and get jobs to make ends meet. Morticia tries to become a preschool teacher, Wednesday and Pugsley sell toxic lemonade and Thing becomes a courier. This is all set in motion by the arrival of a man posing as Fester, the executor of the Addams estate as the elder brother of Gomez.
error: a man posing as Fester
correction: Um, actually, he wasn't merely posing. Gordon is revealed to be the real Fester, long thought lost and suffering from amnesia - so the 'impostor' turns out to have been the genuine Fester all along.
title: The Addams Family

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In this animated series the titular aardvark and his family confusingly keep a pet dog despite the rest of the cast being anthropomorphic animals. The characters include Binky the bulldog, Buster the rabbit, Brain the bear, Francine the monkey and Prunella the parrot.
error: Prunella the parrot
correction: Um, actually, Prunella is a poodle, not a parrot. For all its menagerie of animal characters, Arthur has no bird people in its cast at all.
title: Arthur

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: White Christmas, a 2014 Black Mirror Christmas special, depicts Jon Hamm's Matt and Rafe Spall's Joe in an isolated cabin on Christmas Day. Despite living together for years, they have rarely interacted. It is later revealed that these are 'cookies', a form of digital clone often used as personal assistants. Both are confined to the cabin as punishment for their crimes: Joe for the murder of his ex-father-in-law, and Matt for using people's Z-Eyes to voyeuristically watch them have sex.
error: Both are confined to the cabin as punishment for their crimes
correction: Um, actually, only Joe is being punished. Matt is a cookie coaxing a confession out of Joe to escape prosecution over a client's death; he is freed afterwards, though registered as a sex offender and 'blocked' by everyone for the voyeurism.
title: Black Mirror

author: By ashw92
phrase_text: In The Curse of the Black Pearl, Hector Barbossa captains the titular ship. Thanks to a curse, the pirates become undead who can feel neither pleasure nor pain. To lift the curse they need to locate and return all 882 pieces of Aztec gold to the Isle of Tortuga, along with a blood offering from each of the coins' original owners, or a living relative.
error: the Isle of Tortuga
correction: Um, actually, the gold had to be returned to its stone chest on Isla de Muerta. Tortuga is the lawless pirate port where Jack Sparrow recruits his crew, not the resting place of the cursed treasure.
title: Pirates of the Caribbean

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: The Victoria line is unusual among London Underground lines - it is often cited as the only tube line with more than two stops to have an interchange at every one of its stations. Running between Brixton and Walthamstow Central, it serves busy hubs including Victoria, Green Park and King's Cross St Pancras.
error: the only tube line with more than two stops to have an interchange at every one of its stations
correction: Um, actually, that isn't quite true - Pimlico is the only station on the Victoria line with no interchange recognised by TfL, sitting on its own between Victoria and Vauxhall.
title: London Underground

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: In May 2022, TfL officially opened the central section of the Elizabeth line, but one station - Tottenham Court Road - had not been completed on time, held up by tunnelling issues, changing fire-safety standards and the COVID-19 pandemic.
error: Tottenham Court Road
correction: Um, actually, the delayed station was Bond Street, not Tottenham Court Road. It finally opened on 24 October 2022, roughly 5 months after the rest of the central Elizabeth line.
title: Elizabeth Line

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: Near the climax of "The Truman Show," Marlon climbs out of the tunnel Truman dug from inside a cupboard and looks straight down a hidden camera to tell Christof "He's gone." It's the same spot where, earlier in the film, Truman had been gardening while Meryl cheerfully advertised a new "Elk Rotary" to him - and really to the viewers at home.
error: "Elk Rotary"
correction: Um, actually, there's no "Elk Rotary" - Meryl was plugging the "Chef's Pal," a dicer, grater and peeler in one that never needs sharpening and is dishwasher safe.
title: The Truman Show

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: In a flashback to Truman's college days in "The Truman Show," he is drawn to Sylvia, who goes by the stage name Lauren. Insisting it has to happen now, she slips away from the cameras with him, out to the forest bordering Seahaven for a brief, authentic moment together. A man Sylvia has never met soon arrives to drag her away - off to Fiji - leaving behind only her red sweater.
error: the forest bordering Seahaven
correction: Um, actually, they don't run to a forest - Truman leads Sylvia down to the beach, dodging the cameras so deftly that it foreshadows his eventual escape by sea.
title: The Truman Show

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: In Taylor Swift's "Death by a Thousand Cuts," she likens a breakup to a slow, painful death. In the bridge she names "My heart, my hips, my body, my love" as the parts of her this person irreversibly touched, then lists "My time, my wine, my spirit, my fuss" as she hunts for a part of herself they didn't take up.
error: "My time, my wine, my spirit, my fuss"
correction: Um, actually, the lyric is "my spirit, my trust" - not "my fuss." She's mourning the trust this person took from her, which fits the song's theme of a love that quietly bled her dry.
title: Taylor Swift

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: Taylor Swift's "the last great american dynasty" tells the story of Rebekah Harkness, a real socialite and arts patron who once owned the Rhode Island mansion Swift later bought. Recounting Rebekah's feud with her neighbours, the song claims "She stole his dog and dyed it tangerine."
error: "She stole his dog and dyed it tangerine."
correction: Um, actually, the lyric is "She stole his dog and dyed it key lime green" - not tangerine. It's one of the song's most vivid snapshots of Rebekah's scandalous reputation.
title: Taylor Swift

author: By #SuperFinnee
phrase_text: On April 1, 2016, Apple Music released an ad in which Taylor Swift runs on a treadmill while expertly rapping along to Drake's "One Dance." Mid-song she trips and tumbles off the treadmill, then keeps rapping from the floor. Drake later released his own spot, dancing along to Swift's "Bad Blood" in the gym.
error: Drake's "One Dance."
correction: Um, actually, she was rapping Drake and Future's "Jumpman," not "One Dance." The ad was such a hit it reportedly boosted the song's iTunes sales by over 400%.
title: Taylor Swift

author: By caoenqi
phrase_text: Long before "The Traitors" hit our screens, "The Mole" was one of the first mainstream reality competitions built around a hidden-traitor mechanic. Contestants complete missions to add money to a shared pot while being secretly sabotaged by the titular "Mole" - a player chosen and coached by production, who wins any money the group fails to earn. Each episode, eliminations are decided by a quiz about the Mole, with the lowest scorer sent home.
error: who wins any money the group fails to earn
correction: Um, actually, the Mole doesn't pocket the group's lost money. They're a paid plant working for production - sabotaging purely to stay hidden - and can never win the prize pot themselves.
title: The Mole

author: By ragingdawn
phrase_text: In "Monster Hunter: World," the arsenal runs from the greatsword to the sword and shield to the switch axe - and even a musical instrument. The hunting horn is a blunt weapon that swings like a hammer, but it also plays songs to support the team, buffing every nearby hunter by boosting attack, shoring up defense, negating poison, raising movement speed, and granting mind's eye so attacks won't bounce off tough monster parts.
error: raising movement speed, and granting mind's eye so attacks won't bounce off tough monster parts
correction: Um, actually, those last two come from the "Self-Improvement" song, which only buffs the horn player - not the rest of the party. The attack, defense, and poison melodies help everyone, but the movement speed and mind's eye boost is yours alone.
title: Monster Hunter: World

author: By Bracht
phrase_text: In the 11th episode of Glee's 4th season, the New Directions group performs a surprisingly mellow cover of Sir Mix-a-Lot's raunchy hit "Baby Got Back" - so gentle it could pass for a love song. Fans quickly noticed it copied an arrangement indie musician Jonathan Coulton had released 7 years earlier, right down to the line "Johnny C.'s in trouble," which Coulton had swapped in for "Mix-a-Lot's in trouble." Glee never credited him, even after selling the track on iTunes.
error: the New Directions group performs
correction: Um, actually, it wasn't New Directions - the number was performed by the Adam's Apples, a rival glee club, in the episode "Sadie Hawkins." The protagonist choir never sang it.
title: Glee

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: The acclaimed board game "Root" pits fully asymmetric animal factions - from adventuring raccoons to terrorist crows, diasporic toads and peace-advocating bats - in a sprawling war for the Woodland. Among them are the industrialist cats of the Marquise de Cat, the forest's original rulers, who criss-cross their ancestral homeland raising sawmills and workshops.
error: the forest's original rulers, who criss-cross their ancestral homeland
correction: Um, actually, the cats are the one faction that isn't native to the Woodland. The Marquise de Cat is a foreign empire that invaded during the chaos after the Eyrie Dynasties' civil war and now occupies the forest, industrializing it to serve her own ambitions - it's the deposed birds, not the cats, who are the land's old rulers.
title: Root

author: By apetopac
phrase_text: Beyond its armless title character, the web cartoon "Homestar Runner" fills Free Country, USA with all sorts of odd creatures. Among its dozen or so regulars are Strong Bad, the boxing-gloved, wrestling-masked antagonist-turned-nuisance; Pom Pom, whom Homestar calls his "best friend and confidant" and the town hunk; and Marzipan, Homestar's long-suffering girlfriend.
error: "best friend and confidant"
correction: Um, actually, Homestar calls Pom Pom his "best friend and concubine" - then immediately admits he should probably look up what that word means. It's one of his many malapropisms.
title: Homestar Runner

author: By MisterBrick42
phrase_text: In "Content Warning," an April Fools' parody of "Lethal Company," you and a gang of content creators take a diving bell down to the Old World to film monsters for clout - among them a zombie snail, a ghost with a knife, a statue that only moves when you aren't looking, and a cage that traps a teammate and forces you to solve a captcha to free them.
error: a statue that only moves when you aren't looking, and a cage that traps a teammate and forces you to solve a captcha to free them
correction: Um, actually, those are the same monster - The Weeping. It's a caged, statue-like entity that only creeps forward when no one is watching and snares a player behind a captcha. Two entries, one creature.
title: Content Warning

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: WWE's King of the Ring, while not held every year during its existence, has been a long-standing tournament since 1993 when it was first won by Bret "Hitman" Hart. Since then, Kings of the Ring have included Ken Shamrock, Wade Barrett, Xavier Woods, Billy Gunn (who was known as "Mr. Ass" at the time), and Bret's youngest brother, Owen.
error: long-standing tournament since 1993 when it was first won by Bret "Hitman" Hart
correction: Um, actually, the King of the Ring tournament dates back to 1985; those early editions were held at house shows and thus went largely unpromoted on then-WWF television. The first King of the Ring was Don Muraco.
title: Professional Wrestling

author: By CowAboutThat
phrase_text: Theoretical physicist Dr. Gordon Freeman is the primary protagonist of the Half-Life series, but other spin-off games put you in control of other protagonists. Blue Shift introduces security guard Barney Calhoun, who returns as an NPC in Half-Life 2, and Opposing Force introduces Adrian Shephard, a US Marine Corporal. Both spin-offs take place in the same location and roughly the same time frame as the original game.
error: Blue Shift introduces security guard Barney Calhoun
correction: Um, actually, a Barney-model security guard already appears in the original Half-Life - he can be seen banging on a door during Gordon's opening tram ride - so the character predates his own spin-off.
title: Half-Life

author: By CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In HBO's Game of Thrones spin-off House of the Dragon, based on George R.R. Martin's Fire and Blood, King Viserys Targaryen (played by Paddy Considine) breaks many years of precedent by naming his daughter Rhaenyra (played by Emma D'Arcy) as his successor. He remains firm in this choice, even when he later has a legitimate male heir, Aegon (played by Tom Glynn-Carney), who many believe has a stronger claim to the Iron Throne.
error: naming his daughter Rhaenyra (played by Emma D'Arcy) as his successor
correction: Um, actually, when Viserys names Rhaenyra his heir in the first episode she is played by Milly Alcock; Emma D'Arcy takes over the adult Rhaenyra only from episode 6, after a time skip.
title: House of the Dragon

author: By CowAboutThat
phrase_text: In the hit indie metroidvania "Hollow Knight," players control the Hollow Knight, a vessel designed to have "No mind to think. No will to break. No voice to cry suffering." Yet there are hints it is less hollow than intended: it can equip the Void Heart charm, whose description says it unifies the void under the bearer's will.
error: players control the Hollow Knight
correction: Um, actually, the character you play is not the Hollow Knight but a different vessel called the Knight, or Ghost. The Hollow Knight is a separate sibling vessel, sealed away in the Temple of the Black Egg.
title: Hollow Knight

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: Hearthstone's Battlegrounds mode swaps the usual collectible-card gameplay for an auto-battler: you face 7 other players and assemble a team of minions between rounds. During the recruit phase you know little about a rival's board beyond their most common minion type, and once combat starts you have to direct your minions' attacks on the fly. Placing anywhere in the top 4 counts as a win.
error: once combat starts you have to direct your minions' attacks on the fly
correction: Um, actually, players have no control at all during the combat phase. Once it begins, minions attack automatically in a largely random order while you simply watch.
title: Hearthstone

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: Supermassive Games develops the horror series "The Dark Pictures Anthology." Beginning with their hit game Until Dawn, these titles task players with making decisions for a whole cast of characters, played solo or split among a group of friends. Choose well, and your characters build their relationships while surviving the horrors; choose poorly, and some may not make it to the final credits.
error: Beginning with their hit game Until Dawn
correction: Um, actually, Until Dawn is a separate Supermassive game and is not part of The Dark Pictures Anthology, which actually began in 2019 with Man of Medan.
title: Dark Pictures Anthology

author: By merkon
phrase_text: In the hit YA book series Animorphs, the main villain Visser 3 (later promoted to Visser 1) is a brain-controlling slug named Esplin 9266. Esplin famously controls the Andalite Alloran-Semitur-Corrass, but has also controlled the bodies of multiple Hork-Bajir, a Gedd, and a human.
error: Esplin 9266
correction: Um, actually, Visser Three's designation is Esplin 9466, not 9266. He is the only Yeerk ever to hold an Andalite host, the war-prince Alloran-Semitur-Corrass.
title: Animorphs

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: Myst is an iconic point-and-click adventure game set partly on Myst Island, where Atrus wrote some of his linking books, which can send people to other worlds called "Ages." To beat the game, players must travel to several of these Ages to gather the information needed to finish back on Myst Island.
error: To beat the game, players must travel to several of these Ages
correction: Um, actually, if you already know the fireplace code, you can reach the good ending in a matter of minutes without ever leaving Myst Island - speedrunners exploit exactly this shortcut.
title: Myst

author: By merkon
phrase_text: In the classic YA book series Animorphs, five human teenagers and one Andalite famously turn into animals to fight invading brain slugs. Along the way, a few others also gain the morphing power - a new kid at their school named David, a group of disabled teenagers, a buffalo, an ant, and their vice principal. Unfortunately, everyone else who gains the morphing ability is ultimately killed.
error: and their vice principal
correction: Um, actually, the vice principal, Hedrick Chapman, never received the morphing power. In book 39, a Cape buffalo touches the Escafil device and gains the ability, then acquires Chapman as one of its morphs - but Chapman himself never morphs.
title: Animorphs

author: By CowAboutThat
phrase_text: John-117, better known as Master Chief, is the protagonist of most Halo games. He has secured many crucial victories against the alien coalition known as the Covenant, but at what cost? John, like his fellow Spartan-IIs, was kidnapped as a young child and indoctrinated into obedient military servitude, leaving his caretakers to wonder what happened to him.
error: leaving his caretakers to wonder what happened to him
correction: Um, actually, each abducted Spartan-II child was replaced with a flash clone. The clones soon sickened and died of seemingly natural causes, so their families and caretakers never suspected a kidnapping.
title: Halo

author: By ozyzero
phrase_text: The tabletop actual-play series Dimension 20: A Starstruck Odyssey features a campaign set in a sci-fi universe originally co-created by Elaine Lee and Michael Kaluta, following a ragtag spaceship crew trying to save the galaxy. Notably, it was the first Dimension 20 campaign to use a system other than Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition.
error: it was the first Dimension 20 campaign to use a system other than Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition
correction: Um, actually, Misfits and Magic beat it to that by half a year - it aired in mid-2021 using the Kids on Brooms system, whereas A Starstruck Odyssey did not run until early 2022.
title: Dimension 20

author: By CowAboutThat
phrase_text: The foppish author Volo can be found early in Baldur's Gate 3, forced to serve as a jester for a camp of goblins. Later, he may offer to surgically remove the mind flayer parasite through your eye. If things go wrong and you let him keep working despite repeated warnings, Volo will accidentally carve your eye out, leading to permanent vision penalties.
error: leading to permanent vision penalties
correction: Um, actually, Volo immediately hands you a magical replacement - Volo's Ersatz Eye - which grants permanent See Invisibility. Losing the eye is an upgrade, not a penalty.
title: Baldur's Gate 3

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: Papers, Please casts players as a border-control agent who must verify immigration documents for citizens and foreigners hoping to enter Arstotzka. Follow your corrupt government's constantly changing rules and you'll be promoted to a higher rank; the game also has many other outcomes, from helping a resistance group topple the regime, to watching your family starve, fleeing to Obristan, or being arrested or executed. Glory to Arstotzka!
error: Follow your corrupt government's constantly changing rules and you'll be promoted to a higher rank
correction: Um, actually, obeying the state earns you no promotion - there is no inspector rank to climb. The only promotion, to agent of the New Arstotzka, comes from helping the EZIC resistance; playing by the rules instead just unlocks Endless mode.
title: Papers, Please

author: Fan Submitted
phrase_text: The X-COM series began in 1994 with UFO: Enemy Unknown. The franchise later came to be known as X-COM rather than UFO, and was rebooted after an 11-year hiatus as XCOM: Enemy Unknown. The games put you in control of XCOM, the paramilitary force tasked with fighting off an alien invasion.
error: the paramilitary force tasked with fighting off an alien invasion
correction: Um, actually, not every game is about repelling an invasion. In XCOM 2 the aliens have already won and you lead a resistance to overthrow them, while XCOM: Chimera Squad puts you in charge of a joint human-alien squad policing a city.
title: X-COM
